Container.Name: itsweber-send
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted file sharing in a single Docker container.
THREE DEPLOYMENT MODES:
1) Behind your existing reverse proxy (NPM, Traefik, Caddy-external, Nginx) — DEFAULT for this template. Set REVERSE_PROXY_MODE = true (default), point your proxy at host:3000 over plain HTTP. Set ORIGIN/BASE_URL to your public URL (e.g. https://send.example.com).
2) LAN direct (no public domain). Set REVERSE_PROXY_MODE = false and use the bundled HTTPS port 8443 with the embedded Caddy + self-signed cert.
3) Public with bundled Caddy + Let's Encrypt. Use docker-compose.yml from the repo instead of this template.
FIRST-TIME SETUP (read this before clicking Apply):
The container runs as a non-root user (UID 10001). The host directory you pick for /data must be owned by that UID. Open the Unraid terminal and run:
mkdir -p /mnt/user/appdata/itsweber-send chown -R 10001:10001 /mnt/user/appdata/itsweber-send
Without this, SQLite cannot open the database file and the container will exit on start with a clear error message in the log.
When using REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true behind NPM, set Network to a custom static-IP bridge (e.g. br1) and clear the Port mapping below — your reverse proxy reaches the container directly on its assigned LAN IP.
AFTER THE FIRST SUCCESSFUL APPLY:
Delete the source template file you wgot. Unraid keeps both the source XML and a my- prefixed copy of the applied state, and on Update / Force Update can fall back to the source — overwriting your customisations (network, IP, public domain, port mapping). Run once:
rm /boot/config/plugins/dockerMan/templates-user/itsweber-send.xml
When a new release is out, repeat the wget + Apply + rm cycle.
LICENSE: AGPL-3.0-only. DOCS: https://github.com/ITSWEBER-OFFICIAL/itsweber-send#readme

Config.Name: Reverse Proxy Mode
Config.Description: true: skip embedded Caddy, bind Node on 0.0.0.0:3000 for an external reverse proxy. false: enable embedded Caddy on 8443 with a self-signed cert (LAN-only).

Config.Name: Public URL
Config.Description: The public URL the service is reachable under. Used to build share links and as the cookie scope. Match what your reverse proxy serves.

Config.Name: Base URL
Config.Description: Base URL for the SvelteKit frontend. Set the same value as Public URL.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Pino log verbosity: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal.

Config.Name: Rate Limit Per Minute
Config.Description: Per-IP request limit per minute. Healthcheck endpoints are exempt.

Config.Name: Enable Accounts
Config.Description: Allow optional user accounts on top of anonymous uploads.

Config.Name: LAN Hostname (mode 2 only)
Config.Description: Only used when REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=false: hostname or LAN IP that gets baked into the embedded Caddy's self-signed certificate. Browse via https://<value>:8443.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: SQLite database (shares.db) plus encrypted uploaded blobs. Pre-chown the host directory to 10001:10001 before the first start (see Overview).

Config.Name: HTTP Port (reverse-proxy mode)
Config.Description: Plain HTTP port. Map this when using REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=true and Bridge networking; clear it when using a custom static-IP network like br1.

Config.Name: HTTPS Port (mode 2 only)
Config.Description: Self-signed HTTPS via embedded Caddy. Only map when REVERSE_PROXY_MODE=false.
